findings.title: registerAll() has 3-level nesting; extract single-binding registration
findings.description: registerAll() reaches nesting depth 3 (method body > for-loop > try/catch > if-branch). The loop body contains a try/catch with error wrapping and a success check, making the for-loop body 15 lines. Extracting the per-binding registration into a private method reduces nesting by one level and makes the loop body a single call.

findings.title: Redundant 'as ShortcutAction' casts on DEFAULT_BINDINGS literals
findings.code_snippet:
export const DEFAULT_BINDINGS = [
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+A', action: 'toggle-panel' as ShortcutAction },
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+V', action: 'voice-input' as ShortcutAction },
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+C', action: 'quick-chat' as ShortcutAction },
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+L', action: 'show-log' as ShortcutAction },
] as const
findings.description: Each action value is a string literal that already belongs to the ShortcutAction union type. With 'as const' the array is a readonly tuple of literal types, so the 'as ShortcutAction' casts add no type safety and clutter each line. Using 'satisfies' on the whole array would achieve type-checking without redundant per-element casts.
findings.suggestion:
BEFORE:
export const DEFAULT_BINDINGS = [
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+A', action: 'toggle-panel' as ShortcutAction },
  ...
] as const

AFTER:
export const DEFAULT_BINDINGS = [
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+A', action: 'toggle-panel' },
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+V', action: 'voice-input' },
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+C', action: 'quick-chat' },
  { accelerator: 'CommandOrControl+Shift+L', action: 'show-log' },
] as const satisfies readonly ShortcutBinding[]

WHY: 'satisfies' validates that every action value is a valid ShortcutAction at compile time without widening the literal types or requiring per-element type assertions. Cleaner and catches typos just as well.

positive_observations: All functions are short (<30 lines), consistent with project conventions
Named constant DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS used instead of magic number in clipboard-monitor
Clean guard clauses with early returns throughout (start/stop/registerAll/unregisterAll)
Classes reserved for Imperative Shell (ClipboardMonitor, ShortcutManager) while pure logic (parseAppleScriptOutput) is a plain function -- matches Functional Core / Imperative Shell architecture
Error handling consistently wraps with descriptive messages and cause chains
Types cleanly separated into dedicated types.ts file
